stories.headline: Stocks Halt Selloff; Iran, Israel Exchange Missile Strikes | Bloomberg Brief 6/8/2026
stories.summary: US stocks stage a modest rebound after Friday's selloff. Oil climbs after Iran and Israel trade missile attacks. Sikander Rashid of Brookfield speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie at London Tech Week on the firm's investment in AI infrastructure. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Israel launches strikes on Iran after Tehran fires barrage of missiles
stories.summary: The exchange, after Tehran’s first such strike on Israel in two months, threatened to further complicate efforts to broker a peace deal aimed at ending the war.

stories.headline: IRGC threatens regional energy assets as Iran trades strikes with Israel
stories.summary: An IRGC source warned that any further attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure would trigger strikes on energy assets in the broader region, after Israel struck Iran's Karun petrochemical plant in Khuzestan and the IRGC responded within the hour by hitting facilities in Haifa.

stories.headline: Ukraine, Russia trade fire as Zelenskyy allies back call for direct talks
stories.summary: UK, France, and Germany support Zelenskyy's proposal for a meeting with Putin, as drone attacks continue.

stories.headline: Watch: Why is China's Xi Jinping visiting North Korea?
stories.summary: The Chinese President Xi received a colourful welcome to Pyongyang for his first visit in seven years.

stories.headline: Copper Rebounds After Report Iran Has Ended Israel Strikes
stories.summary: Copper climbed, recouping some of last week’s losses, as Iranian media reported that the country has ended a military operation against Israel.

stories.headline: Xi Jinping arrives in Pyongyang on trip to revitalise China-North Korea ties
stories.summary: Kim Jong-un welcomes Chinese leader on visit to renew relations strained amid Pyongyang’s closeness with RussiaXi Jinping has arrived in North Korea for a two-day trip, his first in nearly seven years, as China’s leader looks to revitalise ties with his junior ally.Footage published by China’s Xinhua state news agency showed an Air China plane carrying Xi and his wife, Peng Liyuan, touching down at Pyongyang’s Sunan international airport. stories.headline: Armenia’s pro-Europe party wins election and cements shift away from Russia
stories.summary: Result strengthens PM Nikol Pashinyan’s drive for deeper integration with Europe despite warnings from MoscowArmenia’s ruling pro-Europe party has won parliamentary elections, confirming the country’s pivot towards Europe and away from its traditional ally, Russia.Final results in the small South Caucasus country showed the prime minister Nikol Pashinyan’s Civil Contract party securing a slim majority, while the Strong Armenia alliance, led by th

stories.headline: China’s Xi Jinping arrives in North Korea on rare state visit
stories.summary: A cheering crowd dressed in festive attire welcomes the Chinese leader at Pyongyang's main square.

stories.headline: Houthis join Iran war fight, threatening Red Sea shipping amid Hormuz closure
stories.summary: Yemen's Houthis declared a ban on Israeli shipping in the Red Sea and claimed a missile strike on Israel, with the Bab el-Mandeb and Hormuz blockades now simultaneously threatening roughly a third of the world's seaborne oil and gas.

stories.headline: Peru election result close as vote counting continues
stories.summary: The race between right-wing Keiko Fujimori and left-wing Roberto Sánchez has been dominated by concerns over crime and political instability.

stories.headline: Peru Bonds Edge Lower as Election Result Hangs in the Balance
stories.summary: Peruvian bonds edged lower as the country’s bitterly contested presidential election remained too close to call with the latest data showing conservative Keiko Fujimori holding the narrowest of leads over leftist Roberto Sánchez.

stories.headline: Iran Announces End to Attacks on Israel Following Trump’s Plea
stories.summary: Iran signaled an end to its current military operations against Israel just after US President Donald Trump said the two sides were looking to agree to an immediate ceasefire following a series of tit-for-tat strikes that threatened to derail talks aimed at ending the war in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran, Israel Looking to Do ‘Immediate Ceasefire’
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump said Israel and Iran are looking to agree to an immediate ceasefire following a series of strikes, while Iran’s central military command says the military operation against Israel has ended, according to semi-official Fars news agency. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Schwab's Martin Says Fed Is Edging Toward a Rate Hike
stories.summary: There's a case for the Federal Reserve to "hike right now,'' and even if policymakers aren't ready yet, the bar to action is definitely getting lower, according to Collin Martin at the Schwab Center for Financial Research. He speaks with Jonathan Ferro and Annmarie Hordern on Bloomberg Television's "Surveillance.'' (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: At least 11 killed in Pakistan-administered Kashmir clashes before rally

stories.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping visits North Korea as Beijing aims to boost ties amid growing Pyongyang-Moscow relations.

stories.summary: Nikol Pashinyan's Civil ​Contract party wins 49.81 percent of ​the vote, the ​Central Election Commission says.

stories.summary: Markets in Europe and Asia were hit by a tech sell-off, and oil rises as Iran and Israel launch attacks on each other.

stories.summary: Buyers step in after Friday fall that rattled global markets

stories.headline: UK’s Crown Estate Plans to Resell Abandoned Wind Farm Site
stories.summary: The British King’s commercial property and land manager is bringing back to market the rights to develop a major wind farm in the Irish Sea after the former developers of the site walked away last year.

stories.headline: SpaceX Set for Historic IPO This Week
stories.summary: Elon Musk wants to build the infrastructure for AI in space — and he's asking investors to help pay for it. The rocket, satellite and now AI company wants to raise around 75 billion dollars in what could become the largest IPO in history, funding everything from its Starship rocket to AI computing power and future data centers in orbit. Bloomberg Tech's Ed Ludlow takes a look at what's at stake. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Fed Faces Rising Rate Hike Expectations, Schwab Center's Martin Says
stories.summary: The bar for a Federal Reserve rate hike is falling as the job market remains robust in the face of stubborn price pressures, according to Collin Martin at the Schwab Center for Financial Research.

stories.headline: Chile’s Tren de Aragua Probe Revives Bank Secrecy Debate
stories.summary: The June arrests of bank employees accused of ties to Venezuela’s Tren de Aragua criminal organization has reignited debate over Chile’s financial secrecy rules, which are among the strictest in the world.

stories.headline: Tate & Lyle Accepts Bid From Ingredion in Loss for London
stories.summary: Ingredion Inc. agreed to buy Tate & Lyle Plc for £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), in a move that marks the end of the UK company’s near-century on the London Stock Exchange.

stories.headline: US Premarket Movers for June 8, 2026
stories.summary: S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.6% at 7:49 a.m. in New York, signaling a modest rebound for stocks after Friday’s slide.

stories.headline: Shekel Flips From Best to Worst, Easing Pressure on Central Bank
stories.summary: Israel’s shekel has swung from the world’s best performer to the worst in a matter of days, easing pressure on the central bank to stem a 14-month rally that’s hurting the country’s exporters.

stories.headline: In Maine, Supporters of Graham Platner Continue to Back His Senate Campaign, With ‘Trepidation’
stories.summary: Many Democrats are sticking by their presumptive Senate nominee Graham Platner. But some have soured, and others are anxious about how recent revelations could affect a close race.

stories.headline: How the Drive to Find a Conspiracy Against Trump Rocked the Justice Dept.
stories.summary: The push to investigate what the president’s allies saw as a “deep state” cabal intent on taking him down set off cascading crises, ended careers and undercut the department’s credibility with judges.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Monsoon season brings vital rainfall to parts of Asia
stories.summary: India declares onset as up to 280mm of rain falls in 72 hours in Kerala, while downpours hit south-west ThailandThe monsoon season has officially begun in parts of Asia, marking the start of a period of enhanced rainfall vital to the region’s economy.The south-west monsoon begins each year as a consequence of a growing temperature difference between the Asian land mass and the Indian Ocean. stories.headline: Hegseth attacks Europe over migration with beach 'invasion' D-Day speech
stories.summary: The US defence secretary was speaking in Normandy, 82 years after allied forces launched their operation to liberate Nazi-occupied north-western Europe.

stories.headline: Hegseth Criticizes Europe Over Migration ‘Invasion’ in D-Day Speech
stories.summary: The remarks from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth reflect many of the Trump administration’s previous assertions on immigrants in Europe, which overlap with the language of European far-right political parties.

stories.headline: Trump walks out of ‘Meet the Press’ interview when challenged over false claims
stories.summary: When pressed by host Kristen Welker, the president cited no evidence for claims about Jan. 6 and elections he said were “rigged.”

stories.headline: Zelensky's close European allies set out five conditions for peace talks

stories.headline: Eriksen conscious after collapsing in Denmark game
stories.summary: Christian Eriksen collapsed during Denmark's friendly against Ukraine but is now conscious, says the Danish Football Association.

stories.headline: Robert Coles, Pulitzer-Winning Child Psychiatrist, Is Dead at 97
stories.summary: His five-volume “Children of Crisis” series, published between 1967 and 1977, drew on his conversations with American children whose voices were not often heard.

stories.headline: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
stories.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

stories.headline: Armenia votes as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
stories.summary: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.

stories.headline: Stocks Gain as Buyers Step In After AI-Led Selloff: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Wall Street staged a comeback as dip buyers emerged, lifting stocks amid renewed enthusiasm over artificial intelligence and bets that a solid economy will keep powering Corporate America.

stories.headline: Ebola spread in central Africa could match 2014 record outbreak, US health officials say

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
stories.summary: The US military strikes Iranian drones and radar sites and Tehran says it has targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.

stories.headline: Insecurity and instability drive voters in Peru's tight presidential race
stories.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.

stories.headline: Ragas and symphonies: Indian maestro Ilaiyaraaja is still reshaping music 50 years on

stories.summary: It is taking place weeks after Xi met the leaders of the US and Russia - two countries that loom large over North Korea's foreign policy.

stories.headline: Norway's crown princess on lung transplant waiting list, palace says
stories.summary: Mette-Marit's condition, which stiffens the lungs, making it hard to breathe, has deteriorated, the royal household says.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill 11 people in Gaza City, medics say
stories.summary: Women and children were among those killed in the attacks on four residential buildings, which Israel's military says killed four Hamas security officials.

stories.headline: Fifa bans all water bottles from World Cup games
stories.summary: Fifa has been warned it is putting fans' health at risk by introducing a bottle ban at this summer's World Cup.

stories.headline: 'Crazy' phone call between Trump and Netanyahu complicates Iran talks

stories.headline: Indonesia's Prabowo fires head of free meals scheme plagued by poisonings

stories.headline: Costly fuel pushes more Indians to buy electric cars but challenges remain
stories.summary: High fuel prices are strengthening the case for EV adoption in the world's third largest auto market.

stories.headline: Canada formally requests 16-year renewal of North American free trade pact
stories.summary: In a letter to his North American counterparts, US-Canada Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said the three countries should renew the USMCA trade deal.

stories.summary: Millenarian thinking permeates business and markets

stories.headline: Wars, sanctions and punishments: Iraq's 40-year World Cup wait

stories.headline: Video shows elaborate drug-smuggling tunnel between US and Mexico
